annotations.label: Vector field (informal)

anchor.snippet: a vector field is an assignment of a vector to each point in a space

annotations.note: Informal notion; Mathlib formalizes the manifold version via `TangentSpace I` with vector fields encoded as functions `(x : M) → TangentSpace I x`.

annotations.label: Tensor field

anchor.snippet: Vector fields are one kind of tensor field

annotations.note: No general tensor field on a manifold is defined in Mathlib (only sections of specific bundles via `ContMDiffSection`).

annotations.label: Vector field on a subset of Euclidean space

anchor.section: Vector fields on subsets of Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: Given a subset S of R n , a vector field is represented by a vector-valued function

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `E → E` directly (no wrapping definition) — the convention is implicit in the `VectorField` namespace operations on functions `E → E`.

annotations.label: Continuous vector field

anchor.section: Vector fields on subsets of Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: If each component of V is continuous, then V is a continuous vector field.

annotations.note: Treated as `Continuous V` (or `ContinuousOn`) for the underlying function; no dedicated vector-field continuity class.

annotations.label: Smooth vector field

anchor.section: Vector fields on subsets of Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: smooth vector fields, meaning that each component is a smooth function

annotations.note: Encoded by applying `ContDiff 𝕜 n` to `V : E → E`; there is no dedicated `SmoothVectorField` predicate.

annotations.label: Counterclockwise rotation vector field

anchor.section: Vector fields on subsets of Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: describes a counterclockwise rotation around the origin

annotations.note: The specific rotation example `(-y, x)` is not formalized as a named declaration.

annotations.label: Module structure on smooth vector fields

anchor.section: Vector fields on subsets of Euclidean space
anchor.snippet: make the smooth vector fields into a module over the ring of smooth functions

annotations.note: No `C^∞(M)`-module structure on smooth vector fields is defined; pointwise `Pi.module` exists but not over the ring of smooth functions specifically.

annotations.label: Contravariant transformation law

anchor.snippet: Such a transformation law is called contravariant

annotations.note: No explicit coordinate-change contravariance law for vector fields formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector field on a manifold

anchor.snippet: a vector field on

annotations.note: Encoded throughout Mathlib as `(x : M) → TangentSpace I x` (used by `VectorField.mlieBracket`, `mpullback`, etc.) but with no top-level `VectorField` definition.

annotations.label: Vector field as section of the tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: a vector field is a section of the tangent bundle

annotations.note: Smooth sections of any vector bundle (including `TangentSpace`) are defined as `ContMDiffSection`; the equivalence with the function-encoding is implicit, not a named lemma.

annotations.label: Vector field as derivation (alternative)

anchor.snippet: An alternative definition

annotations.note: `Derivation` is defined for algebras generally, but the identification with vector fields on a smooth manifold via `C^∞(M)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Smooth/analytic vector fields on manifold

anchor.snippet: then one can make sense of the notion of smooth (analytic) vector fields

annotations.note: Smooth sections of the tangent bundle are captured by `ContMDiffSection I E (TangentSpace I)`.

annotations.label: Wind vector field on Earth

anchor.snippet: A vector field for the movement of air on Earth

annotations.note: Physical example not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Velocity field of a moving fluid

anchor.snippet: Velocity field of a moving fluid

annotations.note: Physical example not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Streamlines, streaklines, pathlines

anchor.snippet: Streamlines, streaklines and pathlines are 3 types of lines

annotations.note: Fluid-dynamic curve concepts not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Magnetic field

anchor.snippet: Magnetic fields . The fieldlines can be revealed using small iron filings.

annotations.note: Physical example not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Electric field from Maxwell's equations

anchor.snippet: the resulting vector field is the electric field

annotations.note: Maxwell's equations and the electric field are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gravitational field

anchor.snippet: A gravitational field generated by any massive object is also a vector field.

annotations.note: Physical example not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gradient field / conservative field

anchor.snippet: is called a gradient field or a conservative field if there exists a real-valued function

annotations.note: The `gradient` of a scalar function is defined, but no `IsConservativeField`/`IsGradientField` predicate on vector fields is given.

annotations.label: Gradient flow

anchor.snippet: The associated flow is called the gradient flow

annotations.note: Gradient flow as a named concept is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Path integral around closed curve in conservative field is zero

anchor.snippet: The path integral along any closed curve

annotations.note: Line integrals of vector fields and the closed-loop vanishing for conservative fields are not formalized.

annotations.label: Central field

anchor.snippet: is called a central field if

annotations.note: Central (radial) vector fields are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Center of the field

anchor.snippet: The point 0 is called the center of the field.

annotations.note: No definition of center of a central field in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central fields are gradient fields

anchor.snippet: A central field is always a gradient field

annotations.note: Not formalized, since neither central nor gradient-field predicates exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Line integral of a vector field

anchor.snippet: the line integral is defined as

annotations.note: No dedicated line integral of a vector field is defined in Mathlib (only complex/contour primitives in `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.HasPrimitives`).

annotations.label: Divergence

anchor.snippet: The divergence of a vector field on Euclidean space is a function

annotations.note: Divergence is only used inline as the sum `∑ i, f' x (Pi.single i 1) i` in divergence-theorem statements; no standalone `divergence` definition exists.

annotations.label: Divergence theorem (reference)

anchor.snippet: a result which is made precise by the divergence theorem

annotations.note: The divergence theorem is formalized over rectangular boxes via `integral_divergence_of_hasFDerivAt_off_countable`.

annotations.label: Curl

anchor.snippet: The curl is an operation which takes a vector field and produces another vector field.

annotations.note: Curl in 3D is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem (reference)

anchor.snippet: This intuitive description is made precise by Stokes' theorem

annotations.note: The classical Stokes' theorem (curl form) is not formalized; only the divergence theorem version is available.

annotations.label: Index of a vector field at a zero

anchor.snippet: The index of a vector field is an integer that helps describe its behaviour around an isolated zero

annotations.note: Index of an isolated zero of a vector field is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Index as degree of map to unit sphere

anchor.snippet: The index of the vector field at the point is the degree of this map.

annotations.note: Neither the degree of a map to a sphere nor the index definition is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Index of vector field as a whole

anchor.snippet: The index of the vector field as a whole is defined when it has just finitely many zeroes.

annotations.note: Total index over finitely many zeros is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: This implies the hairy ball theorem

annotations.note: Hairy ball theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Hopf theorem

anchor.snippet: the Poincaré-Hopf theorem states that the vector field's index is the manifold's Euler characteristic

annotations.note: Poincaré–Hopf is not formalized; Euler characteristic of a manifold is also not available.

annotations.label: Integral curves / trajectories

anchor.snippet: are called integral curves or trajectories

annotations.note: Integral curves of vector fields on manifolds are defined as `IsMIntegralCurve` (and `IsIntegralCurve` in the Euclidean ODE setting).

annotations.label: Picard–Lindelöf theorem (uniqueness of integral curves)

anchor.snippet: By the Picard–Lindelöf theorem

annotations.note: Picard–Lindelöf existence is formalized as `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_eq_picard`, with the manifold companion `exists_isMIntegralCurveAt_of_contMDiffAt`.

annotations.label: Complete vector field

anchor.snippet: is called complete if each of its flow curves exists for all time

annotations.note: No `IsCompleteVectorField` predicate or equivalent is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compactly supported vector fields are complete

anchor.snippet: compactly supported vector fields on a manifold are complete

annotations.note: Not formalized, since completeness of vector fields is not defined.

annotations.label: Smooth vector fields on compact manifolds are complete

anchor.snippet: On a compact manifold without boundary, every smooth vector field is complete.

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-parameter group of diffeomorphisms from a complete vector field

anchor.snippet: the one-parameter group of diffeomorphisms generated by the flow

annotations.note: No construction of the one-parameter group of diffeomorphisms generated by a complete vector field exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incomplete vector field on the real line

anchor.snippet: An example of an incomplete vector field

annotations.note: The standard `x^2` non-complete example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie bracket of vector fields

anchor.snippet: Their failure to commute is described by the Lie bracket of two vector fields

annotations.note: The Lie bracket is `VectorField.mlieBracket` on manifolds (and `VectorField.lieBracket` on normed spaces).

annotations.label: f-relatedness of vector fields

anchor.snippet: we say that

annotations.note: No predicate `IsFRelated` is defined; pullbacks (`VectorField.mpullback`) give the closest infrastructure, with f-relatedness expressible as `mpullback f W = V`.

annotations.label: Lie bracket preserves f-relatedness

anchor.snippet: then the Lie bracket

annotations.note: Naturality of the Lie bracket under pullback is `VectorField.mpullback_mlieBracket`, equivalent to preservation of f-relatedness.

annotations.label: p-vector field

anchor.snippet: yields p -vector fields

annotations.note: Multivector fields (sections of `Λ^p TM`) are not defined as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential k-form

anchor.snippet: yields differential k -forms

annotations.note: Pointwise alternating multilinear maps and exterior derivatives appear in `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.DifferentialForm.VectorField`, but global differential k-forms as bundle sections are not packaged as a single definition.

annotations.label: Vector field on a commutative algebra

anchor.snippet: defining a vector field on a commutative algebra as a derivation on the algebra

annotations.note: Algebraic vector fields as derivations on a commutative algebra are formalized as `Derivation R A M`.
